headline: Nigeria rotation-backfill cycle (8-day gap since May 5 baseline). Canonical composite 23.4 / developing band confirmed — math-hygiene resolution from May-05 baseline has been applied on the data side (dimension means at AWR/EMP/ACT/BND/ACC/SYS/INT 2.0, EQU 1.5 now reconstruct cleanly to 23.4). May 2026 evidence window: HRW 2026 World Report Nigeria chapter remains anchor; sustained Boko Haram/JAS resurgence pattern in Borno; ISWAP northeast operations continuing; Tinubu administration VAPP Act review continuing; mixed institutional signals (Borno Police Commissioner anti-torture warning May 4) carrying forward. No new material score-moving evidence in current cycle. Recommend confirm at 23.4 / developing. Boundary-relevant: composite at 23.4 sits 2.4 points inside developing band (lower threshold 21); not within 1-pt boundary-protocol window but worth flagging proximity for future cycles.
math_hygiene_resolution_carryforward: May-05 baseline flagged published 18.4 vs reconstructed 23.4 (+5.0 discrepancy). Canonical countries.json now shows composite 23.4 — math-hygiene resolution applied on data side. Band placement consequently shifted from critical to developing in canonical data; this cycle confirms the updated state.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.0 confirmed. A1 detection: civil-society and HRW monitoring access sustained; A4 amplification: international reporting active. No material change since May-05 baseline.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.0 confirmed. E1 affective resonance: state-level acknowledgment of suffering scale remains limited; civil-society engagement varies by region. No material change.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.0 confirmed. AC2 proportionality: response to Boko Haram/JAS and IPOB violence remains insufficient relative to documented scale; AC4 resource mobilization: security operations underway with documented civilian-harm pattern.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1.5 confirmed. EQ2 distributional awareness: significant regional inequities persist (northeast humanitarian gaps, Niger Delta environmental issues, ethnic-religious dimensions); EQ4 inclusion: minority and gender-based violence concerns persist. Floor-proximate.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.0 confirmed. B3 scope clarity: VAPP Act review continuing; B5 consent orientation: police-torture documentation persists despite legal framework.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.0 confirmed. AB2 transparency: Borno Police Commissioner May-4 anti-torture warning remains rare institutional acknowledgment; AB4 reparation: mechanisms for security-force-abuse accountability remain limited.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.0 confirmed. S1 causal reasoning: governance reforms via VAPP review continuing; S3 distributional awareness: regional inequities persist.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.0 confirmed. I1 consistency: democratic institutions functioning despite security challenges; I2 non-performance: gap between stated rights commitments and operational practice persists.

key_evidence: HRW World Report 2026 Nigeria chapter (anchor): Boko Haram/JAS resurgence in Borno (May 2025 Mallam Karamti/Kwatandashi 57+ killed, ~70 missing; Sept 2025 Darul Jamal Bama LGA 60+ killed); IPOB gunmen killings in Imo State; ISWAP northeast operations; security force airstrike civilian deaths.
May 4 2026 (carry-over): Borno Police Commissioner public anti-torture warning — institutional acknowledgment.
Tinubu VAPP (Violence Against Persons Prohibition) Act review continuing.
Population scale: ~220M (Africa's most populous country); 8-day rotation gap closure.
2024 State Department Human Rights Reports cumulative baseline: security force abuses, prison conditions, judicial inefficiency.
Math-hygiene resolution: canonical composite now 23.4 matches dimension-mean reconstruction; developing-band placement consequently canonical.

evidence.source: Human Rights Watch World Report 2026 — Nigeria
evidence.finding: Anchor: comprehensive 2025 documentation of Boko Haram/JAS resurgence, ISWAP operations, IPOB Imo killings, security force abuses, airstrike civilian deaths, VAPP Act review.

evidence.source: Daily Post Nigeria — Borno Police Commissioner warns officers against torture (May 4 2026)
evidence.finding: Institutional acknowledgment of torture/rights-abuse risk; rare AWR/ACC/INT mixed signal.

evidence.source: US State Department 2024 Human Rights Reports — Nigeria
evidence.finding: Cumulative baseline documentation of security force abuses, prison conditions, judicial inefficiency.
Cumulative documentation: extrajudicial killings, torture, restrictions on assembly, gender-based violence.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction confirms 23.4 (developing band). Math-hygiene resolution from May-05 baseline applied on data side; canonical state is now math-clean. Confirm at 23.4.

negativeSignals: Sustained Boko Haram/JAS Borno resurgence (117+ killed across 2025 documented attacks); IPOB Imo killings; ISWAP operations; security force airstrike civilian deaths; documented police torture pattern.
positiveSignals: Tinubu VAPP Act review continuing; Borno Police Commissioner May-4 anti-torture warning; functioning federal democratic institutions; civil society and HRW monitoring access.
sector_context: Countries / Africa cluster. Rotation-backfill closure (8-day gap). Cross-reference: Sahel cluster (Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger) and Lake Chad basin. Nigeria's security-force-abuse pattern remains dominant negative signal; institutional engagement (VAPP review, police acknowledgments) remains dominant positive signal.
open_actions_resolved: Math-hygiene resolution carryforward confirmed (canonical 23.4 matches reconstructed 23.4)
open_actions_carried: Watch: any high-court ruling on security-force-abuse accountability
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
